business.headline: Buying a car or bike? The hidden truth behind insurance sold at dealershipsWhen you buy a car or bike, the final bill usually comes as an 'on-road price' that includes insurance. For most buyers, this is accepted without much thought, as it appears to be a standard part of the purchase. But what often goes unnoticed is that this insurance is not mandatory to buy from the dealer.

Middle East war to cut global growth, spike inflation despite truce: World BankThe World Bank's baseline estimate now projects growth in emerging markets and developing economies of 3.65 per cent in 2026, compared to 4 per cent in October, dropping as low as 2.6 per cent in an adverse scenario with a longer-lasting war.

India allows 4 sanctioned tankers carrying Iranian oil to berth at Sikka: ReportIranian oil is often transported by a so-called shadow fleet of vessels that lack internationally recognised insurance and safety certifications. But this requires special permission from the government as exemptions are required under Indian rules for the berthing of ships.

Gold dips, silver slips: Is this the right time to invest or wait?Prices of gold and silver have cooled off after recent highs, but the bigger question remains, is this the right entry point, or is there more downside ahead?

Why April is the best time to reset your budget, SIPs and other investmentsIn Part 2 of our MONEY MAKEOVER series, we explore how April, with salary hikes, bonuses and tax changes, creates the perfect window to pause, reset and move forward with clarity. Let's have a look.

Necessary evolution: SP Group backs Tata Sons listing, seeks RBI clarityIn a statement, Shapoorji Pallonji Mistry said a timely listing is not merely about regulatory compliance, but a step that would reinforce the foundational principles of the Tata Group.

Global shocks may hit every 1–2 years, warns Axis Bank's chief economistAxis Bank chief economist Neelkanth Mishra warns that geopolitical shocks could hit every 1–2 years and explains what India must do to prepare its economy.

Equity inflows jump 55% in March, but debt outflows drag industry negative: AMFIThe data clearly shows that retail investors are becoming more confident and disciplined. While institutional money continues to move in and out of debt funds based on short-term needs, individual investors are steadily increasing their exposure to equities.
Dalal Street records best week in 5 years on Iran-US ceasefire reliefSensex closes 919 points higher, Nifty crosses 24,000; IT stocks tumble

Unclaimed EPF rises to Rs 10,900 crore: Why it's rising and how to claim your moneyYou may not think much about your old PF account after switching jobs, but it could still be holding your money. Unclaimed EPF funds have now crossed Rs 10,900 crore, raising concerns about how easily savings are getting left behind.

TCS shifts focus to AI, infrastructure as demand outlook remains mixedShares of TCS were trading at Rs 2,513 on the BSE, down 2.89% or Rs 74.75, as of 1:27 pm, even as its fourth-quarter performance beat estimates.

RBI wants a 1-hour pause on some UPI payments. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a discussion paper proposing a one-hour delay for certain digital payments. Here's an explainer on how it could work, which transactions it may affect, and what it means for users.

Sending over Rs 10,000 via UPI? RBI may soon introduce a 1-hour delayIf you often use UPI for bigger payments, a change may be on the way. The RBI is looking at adding a one-hour pause for transfers above Rs 10,000 to help prevent online fraud.

Applying for TAN? Income Tax Dept replaces Form 49B with two new formsApplying for a TAN is set to become a lot simpler. The Income Tax Department has replaced the old Form 49B with two new forms, aiming to make the process clearer and easier for different types of applicants. Let's have a look.

Infosys, TCS, HCLTech: Why IT stocks are down despite market rallyThe fall in IT stocks comes even as the Sensex and Nifty moved higher, showing that the weakness is limited to the sector amid concerns around growth and demand.

Sun Pharma shares fall over 4% amid acquisition concernsSun Pharma shares fell over 3.5%, as investor concerns over a potential mega acquisition outweighed optimism, bringing focus on debt, integration risks and the deal's long-term payoff.

TCS shares down 3%: Why the IT giant's stock fell despite strong Q4 result?TCS shares down: The stock was trading at around Rs 2,515.00, down 2.81% as of 10:15 am. It was also among the top losers on both the Nifty50 and the IT index.

Wipro shares rise as board considers buyback after three yearsWipro has informed exchanges that its board will evaluate a share buyback alongside its fourth-quarter results on April 16, bringing the focus back on shareholder returns.
Sensex ends 900 points higher: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 687.99 points to 77,319.64, while the NSE Nifty50 added 211.60 points to 23,986.70 as of 9:58 am.
Ola Electric shares continue to rise, up 6% today. Here's whyAt 9:44 am, Ola Electric shares were trading at Rs 38.71, up Rs 2.37 or 6.52%. Yesterday, the stock closed nearly 20% higher.

entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 24: No competition for Ranveer Singh's film yet Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge continues its steady box office run on Day 24 across India and overseas. The Ranveer Singh-starrer is edging towards becoming one of the top 3 Indian all-time grossers.

Euphoria 3 star Zendaya recalls draining war zone scene that left her bruised Zendaya opened up on the draining war zone scene in Euphoria that left her bruised. The third season of the series also features actors Sydney Sweeney and Jacob Elordi among others.

Jana Nayagan leak claims baseless; CBFC explains film's security protocols The CBFC (Central Board of Film Certification) rejected claims about Vijay's Jana Nayagan leak as baseless. Issuing a formal clarification, the board highlighted its stringent security protocols.

Hopefully everything will be well: Asha Bhosle's granddaughter Zanai Veteran singer Asha Bhosle has been hospitalised due to extreme exhaustion and a chest infection, according to a health update shared by her granddaughter Zanai Bhosle on social media.

Asha Bhosle admitted to Breach Candy Hospital; granddaughter Zanai shares update Playback icon Asha Bhosle was admitted to Breach Candy Hospital, Mumbai, after suffering a cardiac arrest. Her granddaughter Zanai requested privacy as the 92-year-old singer undergoes treatment in the Emergency Medical Services unit.

Viral: Dhurandhar's Madhavan, Arjun Rampal hope Pak hosts Indian awards in old video An old clip, featuring R Madhavan and Arjun Rampal among other Bollywood stars, shows them hoping Pakistan gets to host an Indian awards show. The video has gone viral amid the success of Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

It's Shoe-time: Watch Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, KJo at Devil Wears Prada 2 event Meryl Streep reviewed Karan Johar's footwear during the promotional tour for The Devil Wears Prada 2 in Tokyo. The light-hearted exchange saw Johar foot a query or two from Anne Hathaway, too.

RGV says he never got threats from the underworld: They loved Satya, Company Ram Gopal Varma recalled the underworld's impact on his iconic films, Satya and Company. In turn, he added, the underworld, too, loved his films.

Outcome review: Keanu Reeves shines in black comedy about flaws, self-acceptanceRelease Date:Fri Apr 10, 2026 Outcome review: Jonah Hill's black comedy-drama offers a subdued look at celebrity guilt and human imperfections. The film is elevated by strong performances yet limited by familiar storytelling that doesn't quite linger.

Happy birthday my momo: Saiyaara's Aneet Padda wishes Mohit Suri with a wild video Aneet Padda marked filmmaker Mohit Suri's birthday with a heartfelt Instagram post. The Saiyaara actor also shared a wonky video, adding a dash of humour to her gratitude for the director.

DDLJ in Academy of Motion Pictures list of top travel romance films. Kajol reacts Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge has been included in The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences' list of top romance films that involve travel. Check out how Kajol reacted to the post.

Supriya Ganesh, Maitreya Ramakrishnan slam troll's colourist, 'kamwali bai' jibe Actors Supriya Ganesh and Maitreyi Ramakrishnan hit back at a troll targeting Indian representation in Hollywood. They called out the stereotypes highlighted in a post, also involving Charithra Chandran and Shabana Azeez.

Had a calling: Sara Arjun attends Bhasma Aarti in Ujjain amid Dhurandhar 2 success As Dhurandhar 2 continues to break records at the box office, actor Sara Arjun visited the Mahakaleshwar Temple in Ujjain on Saturday. She attended the Bhasma Aarti and called the experience overwhelming.

Jana Nayagan actor Pooja Hegde hurt by leak: Watch Vijay's last film in theatres Jana Nayagan actor Pooja Hegde reacted to the film's leak by calling it disheartening and a blow to the efforts of the entire team. Urging fans to avoid piracy, she appealed to audiences to watch Vijay's final film the right way.

Ben Affleck gives Jennifer Lopez his share of $60M Beverly Hills home: Report Ben Affleck and Jennifer Lopez's divorce proceedings took a new turn with a fresh update about their Beverly Hills home. The development comes nearly a year after Lopez spoke about the emotional toll of their split.

Jana Nayagan leak: AR Murugadoss, Karthi, Pa Ranjith slam piracy; demand action The leak of Vijay's Jana Nayagan has triggered strong reactions across the South film industry. Actor Karthi and filmmakers AR Murugadoss and Pa Ranjith, among others, have urged audiences to support the film through official platforms.

Can Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana usher Bollywood's Avatar era with VFX blitz? From Koi... Mil Gaya in 2003 to the upcoming Ramayana, VFX in Bollywood promises to take a next-level leap. With a lavish budget across two films and collaboration with Oscar-winning global VFX giant DNEG, Nitesh Tiwari's directorial could be the boost Hindi cinema needs.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 23: Ranveer's film nears Rs 1,700 crore mark globally Dhurandhar: The Revenge is inching closer to the Rs 1,700 crore mark worldwide by continuing its strong box office run into week four. The Ranveer Singh-starrer is now eyeing a spot among the top 3 highest-grossing Indian films of all time.

Real star: Wheelchair cricketer Bhima Khunti hails Ranveer Singh's humility Wheelchair cricketer Bhima Khunti shared his memorable meeting with Ranveer Singh, praising the actor's humility. Here's what he said.

Trump warns China of 'big problems' if it provides air defence systems to IranDonald Trump warned China would face "big problems" if it sends weapons to Iran, after US intelligence suggested Beijing may secretly route air defence systems through third countries. China denied the allegation, calling it baseless.

Hormuz standoff dominates US–Iran talks in Pak as fragile ceasefire hangs in balanceIran has effectively restricted access to the passage in recent weeks, disrupting global energy flows and contributing to a sharp spike in oil prices. The United States has made reopening the strait a top priority, viewing uninterrupted transit as essential to global economic stability.

I will quickly learn Spanish. It won't take too long. I'm good at language and I will go to Venezuela. I'm going to run for president." President Donald J. Trump, April 5, 2026, Washington D.C.

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

Indian techie requests flexible hours to care for newborn, US manager's reply is viralAn Indian techie's honest message about wanting flexible working hours to care for his newborn baby was met with warmth by his American manager, emphasising trust and a healthy work culture.

Live: Excessive US demands hindered agreement, says Iranian mediaUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Talks between Iran and the US faltered on Sunday after US Vice President JD Vance said that Tehran did not accept US's terms to end the war. Vance has reportedly left for the US following the talks hitting a roadblock. The first phase of US-Iran talks in Islamabad concluded with both sides exchanging written texts to confirm agreements, even as earlier face-to-face meetings marked the start of direct negotiations. Limited progress was reported on key issues, including possible restrictions on strikes in southern Lebanon and movement on unfreezing Iranian assets. Iran has maintained hard red lines, insisting on a Lebanon ceasefire and broader conditions, while tensions remain high with US mine-clearing operations underway in the Strait of Hormuz and warnings issued to American vessels.

Indian-born cheetah in Kuno welcomes 4 cubs, the 1st recorded birth in the wildA 25-month-old Indian-born female cheetah has given birth to four cubs in Kuno National Park. This marks the first wild birth by a second-generation cheetah since the 2022 reintroduction.

Trump says 'no idea' how Iran talks will go as US-Iran meeting opens in PakistanThe US president said he would know shortly if he felt Iran was acting in good faith about resolving the war. When asked how negotiations would go, Trump said: "I have no idea."

Tristan Stubbs lashes out after glove change denial proves costly in Chennai Tristan Stubbs was left frustrated after being denied a glove change in humid Chennai, a moment that disrupted his rhythm and proved costly as Delhi Capitals fell short against CSK.

Ton of relief: Sanju Samson arrives in yellow as CSK beat DC to end home curse After a string of low-key outings, Sanju Samson returned to form in emphatic fashion, smashing a superb hundred as Chennai Super Kings, backed by a sharp effort from their bowlers, outclassed Delhi Capitals by 23 runs to register their first win of the Indian Premier League 2026 on Saturday.

Sooryavanshi will make people forget Abhishek: Ex-Pak cricketer's bizarre claim Tanveer Ahmed's bold claim on Vaibhav Suryavanshi eclipsing Abhishek Sharma sparks debate as the 15-year-old continues his stunning IPL 2026 rise with fearless batting displays.

Archery World Cup: Compound women salvage pride with team gold after recurve flop The Indian compound women's trio of Jyothi Surekha Vennam, Madhura Dhamangaonkar and Pragati edged past the USA 233-232 to clinch the gold medal at the Archery World Cup Stage 1 here on Saturday.

Sarfaraz's blinder sends Chepauk in frenzy, Gurjapneet takes wicket on IPL debutSarfaraz Khan's stunning catch handed Gurjapneet Singh a wicket on his first IPL ball, derailing Delhi Capitals' chase and turning momentum firmly in Chennai Super Kings' favour.

Jasprit Bumrah's drought no concern for MI, Pollard says defence matters tooJasprit Bumrah's quiet start hasn't rattled MI, with Kieron Pollard backing his impact beyond wickets ahead of the RCB clash at Wankhede.

Ayush Mhatre becomes 2nd CSK player to be retired out after scoring fiftyCSK batter Ayush Mhatre was retired out on 59 off 36 balls against Delhi Capitals at the MA Chidambaram Stadium on Saturday night, after bringing up his second half-century of the season.

Sanju Samson celebrates his first CSK hundred with Rajnikanth's Padayappa saluteSanju Samson has made a sensational comeback to form as he scored his maiden IPL hundred for Chennai Super Kings during the clash against Delhi Capitals on Saturday, April 11.

Ishan Kishan laments SRH's poor finish with the bat in six-wicket loss to PBKSIshan Kishan admitted that Sunrisers Hyderabad fell well short of a potential 250 total despite a flying start, pointing to poor execution with the bat in the death overs. Punjab Kings, led by Shreyas Iyer, capitalised on that lapse with a composed chase to seal a six-wicket win.

Advantage Manchester City? Arsenal stumble in title race with Bournemouth lossAlex Scott's decisive strike halts Arsenal's momentum and tightens the title race as the Gunners lose 1-2 to Bournemouth at the Emirates Stadium. This means that the advantage is with Manchester City, who have two games in hand over Arsenal.

Animesh Kujur overcomes false start setback, shines with 100m-200m doubleStar sprinter Animesh Kujur of Odisha dominated the Indian Athletics Series one-day meet, winning both the 100m and 200m events in a competitive outing on Saturday.

Auqib Nabi finally makes IPL debut for DC, Dewald Brevis returns for CSKAuqib Nabi finally gets the chance to make his IPL debut for DC as he is part of the lineup to face off against CSK at the Chepauk on Saturday, April 11.

LSG assistant coach Lance Klusener credits bowling for good IPL startLucknow Super Giants assistant coach Lance Klusener believes the team's overseas batters are "sleeping giants" who will peak at the right time, while crediting the bowling unit for driving their recent wins. Strong contributions from the Indian batting and bowling core have kept LSG competitive, even as their overseas stars are yet to hit top gear.

Watch: Athletics Federation under fire after man walks onto track during raceA man walking onto the track during a junior 200m race in New Delhi has sparked outrage, with fans slamming the Athletics Federation of India over safety lapses and poor event management.

CSK vs DC Highlights: Chennai open account with 23-run win over DelhiChennai Super Kings (CSK) vs Delhi Capitals (DC) Highlights, IPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have opened their account with a sensational win at the Chepauk to stop their rot and move off the bottom of the table. Sanju Samson's hundred and Jamie Overton's four-wicket haul sealed the win for the side, who can look up and hope to maintain the momentum.

Catch of IPL 2026? Jansen pulls off a one-handed screamer to dismiss Ishan KishanMarco Jansen pulled off a sensational one-handed catch at the boundary to dismiss Ishan Kishan in PBKS vs SRH, swinging the momentum firmly in Punjab's favour in Mullanpur.

Champion Rashid Khan doesn't need to reinvent to find form: GT assistant coachGT assistant coach Vijay Dahiya claimed that a champion bowler like Rashid Khan doesn't need to reinvent himself to regain his form. Rashid has shown signs of form during GT's win over DC in the IPL 2026.

Abhishek stuns PBKS with 28-ball 74 as SRH post third-highest powerplay total in IPLAbhishek Sharma lit up Mullanpur with a breathtaking display of power-hitting as Sunrisers Hyderabad stormed to the joint third-highest powerplay total in the history of the Indian Premier League on Saturday, April 11.

After his win over the World No. 1, the spotlight is firmly on what comes next.

IPL 2026: Natarajan opens up on injury comeback, credits DC for strong supportT. Natarajan has credited Delhi Capitals' structured support system for helping him recover from a collarbone injury and regain form in IPL 2025. His improved fitness and yorker execution have made him a key figure in the franchise's bowling attack this season.

Zero points, no Dhoni: Can CSK fading fortress survive a DC raid?IPL 2026, CSK vs DC preview: A winless Chennai Super Kings take on a bruised Delhi Capitals at Chepauk on Saturday, desperate to snap a six-match home losing streak. Dewald Brevis is likely to return from injury, while CSK may have to wait longer for the comeback of their talisman, MS Dhoni.

Daryl Mitchell calls out Usman Tariq pause, R Ashwin praises New Zealand batterR Ashwin praised Daryl Mitchell for objecting to Usman Tariq's delivery pause during the PSL clash between Quetta Gladiators and Rawalpindi. He said batters are within their rights to step away if the pause is inconsistent and urged umpires and captains to address the issue.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna.
